title: Make varlink API more idiomatic
body:
See individual commits. This is a first round of fixes I've found when I started working with this API for [cockpit-podman](https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman). I have some more, but these were the ones I could easily implement.

I hope that you don't consider the varlink API to be stable, yet? As far as I know, cockpit-podman is the only user so far and I'm fine updating that on the next release.

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
The Varlink API is far from stable. Changes are quite welcome.
Hm, I don't understand the latest error on the "gating" check. It says "9 commits to fix", but not what the fixes should be...

@larskarlitski: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2264#issuecomment-460395698):

>Hm, I don't understand the latest error on the "gating" check. It says "9 commits to fix", but not what the fixes should be...


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Gitvalidation is complaining that you need a signoff on your commits

@baude Will the time format changes here mean we need changes in `adapter` to read times back in as RFC3339?

/ok-to-test
bot, add author to whitelist

@larskarlitski cockpit is not the only user of the API ... the remote client for darwin I am writing is the a major one.  Unfortunately we don't quite have integration tests running for those yet as we are still working on a couple of container related ones.  Until that is resolved, the tests cannot be run.  So you will have to make sure you don't break that either.
please dont remove endpoints that are not implemented.  it is the only list we have for what needs to be done still.
@larskarlitski great start ... i like the changes for images and containers naming. if you can address my comments, that would be great.
/retest
/retest
@larskarlitski Have you had a chance to address @baude Issues?

> Gitvalidation is complaining that you need a signoff on your commits

Thanks. Added those and addressed @baude's comments. Thanks for the reviews!

> please dont remove endpoints that are not implemented. it is the only list we have for what needs to be done still.

We can't commit to a stable API if some of the functions return a `NotImplemented` type. Better to leave out the things that aren't there yet. to track missing functions, would you be ok with me adding a list in a `TODO` file or similar?

> > Gitvalidation is complaining that you need a signoff on your commits
> 
> Thanks. Added those and addressed @baude's comments. Thanks for the reviews!
> 
> > please dont remove endpoints that are not implemented. it is the only list we have for what needs to be done still.
> 
> We can't commit to a stable API if some of the functions return a `NotImplemented` type. Better to leave out the things that aren't there yet. to track missing functions, would you be ok with me adding a list in a `TODO` file or similar?

there are several i dont mind pruning.  Ill remove those in a different PR.  Can we compromise and instead of removing, comment them out?

> there are several i dont mind pruning. Ill remove those in a different PR. Can we compromise and instead of removing, comment them out?

Good idea! Done.

Are these test failures related to my changes? I can't see how they would be...

Restarted one of the cirrus tests to see if this is just flakes, or we have a real issue

@baude Mind taking another look at this?
kind of baffled by this one; the tests look like their legitimately failing?

> kind of baffled by this one; the tests look like their legitimately failing?

I don't understand all the tests and it's hard to get them working locally. I've found one place where the remoteClient panics in `splitStringDate`, because it still assumed the old time format. I could remove this function, because we're now just passing along standard time stamps.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2313) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@baude @jwhonce Tests are passing PTAL
please rebase and I will merge it @larskarlitski
Rebased.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #2315) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Rebased, again.
@larskarlitski I will watch your PR .. .btw, join us on #podman on freenode!
/approved
/lgtm
/approve
